topic: Moscato d'Asti fermentation arrest and style
trigger_keywords: baja graduacion alcoholica dulzor y aromas florales

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Aromatic Muscat must is fermented under pressure and the fermentation is stopped before all grape sugar is consumed.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Chilling and filtration halt yeast activity, retaining grape sugar and limiting alcohol production while preserving primary Muscat aromas and some dissolved carbon dioxide.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine is lightly sparkling, sweet, low in alcohol and strongly floral and grapey.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: arrested fermentation explains the combination of residual sweetness, low alcohol and aromatic fruit in Moscato d'Asti.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
